title: Handshake vs HeyReach — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and HeyReach for LinkedIn automation. See features, pricing, safety, multi-sender rotation, and team collaboration side-by-side.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a cloud-based LinkedIn automation platform built from the ground up for multi-sender rotation. Teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies on every plan. Pricing is based on sender accounts — not per-seat — with unlimited team seats included. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender, $199/month for 5 senders, and $299/month for 10 senders.
overview.competitor: HeyReach is a cloud-based LinkedIn automation platform specifically designed for agencies and teams managing multiple LinkedIn accounts. It offers three plans: Starter at $79/sender/month (with discounts at 10+ accounts), Agency at $999/month for up to 50 senders, and Unlimited at $1,999/month for unlimited senders (capped at 500 under fair-use policy). HeyReach focuses on sender rotation, unified inbox, and campaign management — positioning itself as the go-to tool for high-volume LinkedIn outreach operations. The pricing model is per LinkedIn sender, not per user.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — automatic lead distribution across all connected sender accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Built-in sender rotation — a core feature matching Handshake's approach

featureComparison.handshake: Automated warmup scheduling with gradual 3-week ramp-up for new accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Warmup features available with gradual limit increases

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox across all sender accounts with conversation management
featureComparison.competitor: Unified inbox across all connected LinkedIn accounts

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan — managers, SDRs, and clients access for free
featureComparison.competitor: Unlimited users per workspace — no per-user charges

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included on every plan, matched to sender geography
featureComparison.competitor: Cloud-based with safety features, but proxy infrastructure details less specified

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in A/B testing on all plans
featureComparison.competitor: A/B testing available for message sequences

featureComparison.handshake: Advanced analytics with acceptance rates, reply tracking, sender performance metrics
featureComparison.competitor: Campaign analytics with detailed performance reporting

sections.handshakeWins:
Handshake and HeyReach are the closest competitors in this space — both are cloud-based, both focus on multi-sender rotation, both offer unified inboxes, and both charge per sender rather than per user. This makes the comparison genuinely interesting because the differences are in the details rather than fundamental architecture.

Handshake's primary advantage is pricing at the small-to-mid scale where most teams operate. For 1-10 senders, Handshake is consistently 30-50% cheaper:

- 1 sender: $69/mo vs. $79/mo
- 5 senders: $199/mo vs. $395/mo (a $196/mo savings)
- 10 senders: $299/mo vs. $590/mo (a $291/mo savings)

For a team running 5 sender accounts, that's $2,352/year saved by choosing Handshake. At 10 senders, it's $3,492/year.

Handshake explicitly includes premium residential proxies matched to each sender's geographic location on every plan. This is a transparency advantage — you know exactly what safety infrastructure is protecting your accounts. HeyReach operates safely in the cloud but is less specific about its proxy infrastructure.

Handshake's warmup automation is well-documented with a clear 3-week ramp-up protocol. For teams onboarding new sender accounts regularly, this reduces the operational burden of manually managing limits during the warmup period.
sections.competitorWins:
HeyReach has a compelling advantage at high volume. Their Agency plan at $999/month for up to 50 senders is extremely competitive — that's $20/sender/month at full capacity. The Unlimited plan at $1,999/month for up to 500 senders drops to $4/sender/month.

For large agencies managing 30-50+ LinkedIn accounts across multiple clients, HeyReach's flat-rate Agency plan can be significantly cheaper than Handshake's per-sender pricing at that scale. If you're running 50 sender accounts, $999/month on HeyReach vs. custom pricing on Handshake is a clear advantage for budget-conscious large agencies.

HeyReach has also built strong brand recognition in the agency space. Their marketing, content, and community are specifically tuned for agencies managing high-volume LinkedIn operations. The platform's UX is designed around agency workflows — managing multiple clients, multiple campaigns per client, and dozens of sender accounts from a single workspace.

HeyReach has been in the market with multi-sender rotation as a core feature for longer, meaning their rotation algorithms and account management features have had more time to mature through real-world usage at scale.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if you're running 1-15 sender accounts and want the best price-to-value ratio. It's ideal for sales teams, small-to-mid agencies, and any team where $199-$299/month for 5-10 senders makes more sense than $395-$590/month. Handshake is also the better choice if you prioritize explicit residential proxy inclusion and transparent safety infrastructure.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose HeyReach if you're a large agency managing 30-50+ LinkedIn sender accounts and the $999/month Agency plan makes economic sense. At that volume, HeyReach's flat-rate pricing delivers exceptional per-sender value. Also consider HeyReach if your agency workflow needs have been validated by their specific feature set and community.
sections.verdict: This is a genuine head-to-head comparison between two tools that took the same architectural approach. For 1-15 senders, Handshake wins on pricing — saving 30-50% compared to HeyReach's per-sender rates. For 50+ senders, HeyReach's flat-rate Agency plan at $999/month is hard to beat. Both offer multi-sender rotation, unified inbox, and team collaboration. The decision comes down to your scale: Handshake for small-to-mid teams, HeyReach for large agencies.

faqs.question: How do Handshake and HeyReach compare on features?
faqs.answer: They're remarkably similar. Both offer multi-sender rotation, unified inbox, warmup automation, A/B testing, and unlimited team seats. The core feature sets are nearly identical — the main differences are in pricing structure and proxy infrastructure transparency.

faqs.question: Which is cheaper — Handshake or HeyReach?
faqs.answer: For 1-15 senders, Handshake is 30-50% cheaper. For 50+ senders, HeyReach's Agency plan at $999/month offers better per-sender economics. The breakeven point is roughly 15-20 senders, depending on Handshake's enterprise pricing.

faqs.question: Does HeyReach include multi-sender rotation?
faqs.answer: Yes. HeyReach is one of the few tools alongside Handshake that offers genuine multi-sender rotation as a core feature. Both tools automatically distribute outreach across connected LinkedIn accounts.

faqs.question: Can I switch from HeyReach to Handshake?
faqs.answer: Yes. Connect your LinkedIn accounts to Handshake and set up new campaigns. Both platforms use similar cloud-based infrastructure, so the transition is straightforward. Most teams get running within a day.

faqs.question: Which tool is better for agencies?
faqs.answer: It depends on agency size. For agencies managing 5-15 client accounts, Handshake's pricing ($199-$299/mo) is more affordable. For large agencies managing 30-50+ accounts, HeyReach's Agency plan ($999/mo) offers better per-sender value.
